claimText: The Mercator map projection is a conformal map that preserves local shapes and angles.
evidenceExcerpt: Conformal map designed in 1569 that preserves local shapes and angles.
examUse: Statement-based question on the properties of map projections.
distractorHooks: Preserves scale and area instead of shapes and angles

claimText: The Mercator projection distorts scale by enlarging landmasses near the poles and shrinking equatorial regions.
evidenceExcerpt: Distorts scale by enlarging landmasses near the poles and shrinking equatorial regions.
examUse: Statement-based question on the limitations of the Mercator projection.
distractorHooks: Enlarges equatorial regions and shrinks polar landmasses
Preserves scale perfectly across all latitudes

claimText: The African Union endorsed the Correct the Map campaign to replace the Mercator projection with the Equal Earth map.
evidenceExcerpt: African Union endorsed the Correct the Map campaign to replace it with the Equal Earth map.
examUse: Factual statement in a question about map projection controversies or African Union initiatives.
distractorHooks: Endorsed by the European Union or United Nations
Campaign aims to replace the Equal Earth map with the Mercator projection
